ogTitle: Jabuk vs James Well: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Jabuk and James Well.
heading: Jabuk vs James Well — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Jabuk, South Australia and James Well, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: James Well has a lower overall climate risk score (40/100) compared to Jabuk (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Jabuk or James Well safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Jabuk has a flood risk score of 58/100, while James Well scores 43/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Jabuk scores 42/100 for bushfire risk. James Well scores 37/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Jabuk or James Well?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. James Well has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
